name: Turoyo

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Turoyo (turo1239).

notes: Skeleton. Turoyo (Ṣurayt), a modern Semitic (Afroasiatic, Central Neo-Aramaic) language written in the Syriac abjad. Consonant letters are mapped to their base IPA values. Vowels are written only with optional pointing and the matres lectionis ⟨ܐ ܘ ܝ⟩, so unpointed text leaves most vowels unrecovered and a high PER floor is expected. Spirantized reflexes of the begadkepat letters, extra phonemes marked by diacritic dots, gemination and stress are left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Turoyo (turo1239)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
